task: I’m planning a routine drive this week and want to make sure I’m not overlooking anything important before I go. If there’s anything that seems worth a second look, I’d like to hear it along with the safer option to consider.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it simple and check these first:

## Worth a second look
- smoke
- overheating
- brake smell
- soft or low tire
- leak under the car
- warning light
- new noise
- brakes feel different

## Safer option
- if it’s **new or changing**, check it before you go
- if it’s **smoke, overheating, brake smell, or a leak**, don’t drive it
- if it’s **just cosmetic or old and unchanged**, it can usually wait

## Before you leave
- tire pressure
- fuel level
- lights
- wipers
- mirrors
- seat and steering wheel
- doors and trunk closed
- spare tire, jack, lug wrench

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page glove-box checklist**.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good. A couple things I’d want on there too: battery condition, coolant level, and whether the spare is actually usable, not just present. Last time I had a tire go bad, the spare was junk too, and that turned a nuisance into a long wait.

Safer option for me would be: if anything is new, getting worse, or smells hot, I’d check it before leaving. If it’s tire pressure or a light bulb, fine — handle it now if easy. If it’s brake feel, overheating, or a fluid leak, I’m not taking the chance.

If you want to tighten it up, make it a short pre-trip list in the order I’d actually check it.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s the tighter order.

## Worth a second look
- smoke
- overheating
- brake smell
- soft or low tire
- leak under the car
- warning light
- new noise
- brakes feel different
- battery weak or old
- coolant low
- spare tire not usable

## Safer option
- if it’s **new, getting worse, or smells hot**, check it before you go
- if it’s **brake feel, overheating, or a leak**, do not drive it
- if it’s **something simple like tire pressure or a bulb**, fix it now if easy
- if the **spare is bad**, do not count on it

## Before you leave
- [ ] walk around the car
- [ ] check tire pressure
- [ ] check fuel level
- [ ] check lights
- [ ] check coolant level
- [ ] check battery condition
- [ ] check wipers / washer fluid
- [ ] make sure spare tire is usable
- [ ] make sure jack and lug wrench are there
- [ ] mirrors set
- [ ] seat and steering wheel adjusted
- [ ] doors and trunk closed
- [ ] registration / insurance card in the car
- [ ] if cold or wet, scrape windows and check defroster

If you want, I can make this into a **very short glove-box version** with just the essentials.

user_simulator.text:
That does it. Battery, coolant, and the spare being usable — that’s the part I didn’t want left out.

Order looks right too. Walk around first, then tires, lights, fluids, battery, and the tools. Simple enough to use without thinking too hard.

I’d keep the rule as: new, getting worse, or smells hot — check it. Brake feel, overheating, leak — don’t go. Tire pressure or a bulb, fix it if it’s easy.

Good. That’s the kind of list I’d actually use.
